understanding.goal_understanding: The objective was to solve each level by moving (and if needed pushing) a block onto the goal tile(s) marked with `+` in the semantic grid, progressing through levels until the engine reports WIN for the whole run.
understanding.mechanics_understanding: On each turn, the currently selected piece (shown as `P`) could be moved one grid cell per action; each cell corresponds to 3 pixels, so moves shift the piece by 3 pixels in the chosen direction. Walls (`#`) block movement, and if a pushable object (`B`) is directly adjacent in the movement direction and the space beyond is free, it can be pushed along with the selected piece. Levels may include multiple controllable pieces: using `CLICK` on the exact pixel/grid coordinate of another selectable switches control (with non-selected selectables shown as `S`). The movement blocked flags are only hints about adjacency and don’t guarantee failure; actual movement depends on collision rules with walls/objects.
